summary: Iteration 2 tested three new features: 6-color edge palette, draggable edge labels, and node resizer. All new features work + regression passes.

issues: Recurring warning: 'It looks like you've created a new nodeTypes or edgeTypes object.' — carried over from iteration 1. Non-blocking.

action_items: (Optional) Silence React Flow nodeTypes/edgeTypes warning — likely triggered because edgeTypes = { custom: CustomEdge } object is created at module scope but CustomEdge closes over useReactFlow(). Wrapping in useMemo inside Canvas would still recreate; the module-level definition should suffice — investigate why RF still warns.
critical_code_review_comments: setColorOnSelected uses `const dashed = !!e.data?.dashed || true;` which is always true — that's fine as documented (color implies dashed) but the || true makes the !! redundant. Consider simplifying to `const dashed = true;`.
CustomEdge uses window mousemove/mouseup listeners in useEffect — cleanup is correct, but drag ignores container zoom pan translations correctly via rf.getZoom(). Good.
App.js still 865 lines — recommend splitting CustomEdge, node renderers, EDGE_COLORS, and toolbar into separate files.
seed_data_creation: None (test-created TEST_ status_checks rows persist harmlessly).
context_for_next_testing_agent: New features verified: (a) All 6 edge-color-{red,purple,blue,green,orange,gray} buttons present & apply correct stroke — verified blue = rgb(62,123,209) via computedStyle, dasharray = '8px, 5px'. (b) NodeResizer renders 8 handles (.react-flow__resize-control) when node selected. (c) Edge labels render as HTML divs with data-testid=edge-label-{id} inside .react-flow__edgelabel-renderer; drag via mouse events changes the CSS translate offset; 'Center label' (data-testid=reset-label) resets transform back to original midpoint. Regression: all 3 tabs, auto-layout LR/TB, add-node, toggle-dashed, reverse-edge, node click side-panel all pass. Backend pytest 4/4 passing.
